narrative.headline: 2020 Chevy Equinox: brakes and engine top owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 281 complaints with NHTSA. Brakes are the biggest concern at 35% of reports, tied to 8 crashes and 5 injuries. Engine trouble is next at 27%, followed by the fuel/propulsion system at 17%, then power train and electrical at 13% each. A few reports involved a fire across the engine, brake, and fuel systems. No deaths were reported.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The top complaint area: 98 reports (35%). Linked to 8 crashes, 5 injuries, and 2 fires, so it is the one to inspect most carefully before buying.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 76 reports (27%), tied to 3 crashes, 3 fires, and 1 injury. Watch for any engine warning, loss of power, or burning smell.

issues.sample: PULLING OUT OF CARWASH.TURNING LEFT IN CARWASH LOT. CAR ENGINE REVED UP, CAR LUNGED FORWARD AT HIGH SPEED. SLAMMED ON BRAKE BUT BRAKE PEDAL WENT RIGHT TO THE FLOOR. CAR THEN SIDE SWIPED CARWASH VACUUM SYSTEM ON PASSENGER SIDE SCRAPING AND DENTING BOTH PASSENGER SIDE DOORS. THREW
issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 48 reports (17%), with 1 crash and 1 fire. Worth a close look at how the car responds to the gas pedal.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 37 reports (13%), tied to 3 crashes. Test how the car drives and shifts under load.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 36 reports (13%), with 1 crash. Check that locks, fobs, and electronics all work.
